# Alert Dialog

A modal dialog that interrupts the user with important content and expects a response.

## Installation

Add the Alert Dialog component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/alert-dialog
```

## Usage

Use an alert dialog only when a decision must be resolved before the current task can continue—for example, before deleting data or discarding unsaved work. Name the consequence in the description and label the action with the operation itself, such as *Delete account*, rather than a vague *Continue*.

```vue
<template>
  <AlertDialog>
    <AlertDialogTrigger as-child>
      <Button variant="outline">
        Show dialog
      </Button>
    </AlertDialogTrigger>
    <AlertDialogContent>
      <AlertDialogHeader>
        <AlertDialogTitle>Are you absolutely sure?</AlertDialogTitle>
        <AlertDialogDescription>
          This action cannot be undone. It will permanently delete your
          account and remove your data from our servers.
        </AlertDialogDescription>
      </AlertDialogHeader>
      <AlertDialogFooter>
        <AlertDialogCancel>Cancel</AlertDialogCancel>
        <AlertDialogAction :class="buttonVariants({ variant: 'destructive' })">
          Delete account
        </AlertDialogAction>
      </AlertDialogFooter>
    </AlertDialogContent>
  </AlertDialog>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  AlertDialog,
  AlertDialogAction,
  AlertDialogCancel,
  AlertDialogContent,
  AlertDialogDescription,
  AlertDialogFooter,
  AlertDialogHeader,
  AlertDialogTitle,
  AlertDialogTrigger,
} from '@/Components/AlertDialog'
import { Button, buttonVariants } from '@/Components/Button'
</script>
```

## Structure and focus

Include one `AlertDialogTitle`, a concise `AlertDialogDescription`, a safe `AlertDialogCancel`, and the final `AlertDialogAction`. The modal primitive moves focus into the dialog, keeps keyboard interaction inside it while open, and returns focus after it closes. Keep the cancel action available unless the user truly has no choice.

`AlertDialogTrigger` supports `as-child` so an existing button can open the dialog without an extra wrapper. The child must remain focusable and must forward the merged attributes described in the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition).

## Controlled state

Bind `v-model:open` for normal Vue-controlled state, or pass a StackTrace `useToggle()` controller to the `control` prop. Prefer the declarative trigger for ordinary local interactions; use controlled state when an external event needs to open or close the dialog.

## When to use something else

Do not interrupt people for routine information or reversible actions. Use an inline message or toast for status, and use Dialog for forms or focused content that is not a high-consequence decision.

> **Centralize application-wide confirmations: [Confirmation Dialog](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/confirmation-dialog)**
>
> When many unrelated features need to request the same kind of programmatic confirmation, Confirmation Dialog provides the StackTrace-specific composable API and processing behavior.

Hosted documentation: [Alert Dialog](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/alert-dialog)
